title: galium verum subsp. euverum
intro: Galium verum subsp. euverum, also known as the yellow bedstraw, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Rubiaceae and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it useful for treating conditions such as wounds, fever, and kidney stones. The health benefits of Galium verum subsp. euverum include its ability to reduce swelling, promote healing, and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
